rules.name: no-invalid-regexp
rules.notes: Rust/Oxc RegExp constructor and flag validation port of eslint-plugin-regexp/no-invalid-regexp; duplicate flags, incompatible u/v flags, and constructor parse errors covered in Vitest.

rules.name: no-empty-character-class
rules.notes: Rust pattern scanner port of eslint-plugin-regexp/no-empty-character-class for empty character classes in literals and static RegExp constructors.

rules.name: no-empty-group
rules.notes: Rust pattern scanner port of eslint-plugin-regexp/no-empty-group for empty capturing and non-capturing groups.

rules.name: no-empty-capturing-group
rules.notes: Rust pattern scanner port of eslint-plugin-regexp/no-empty-capturing-group for empty capturing groups.

rules.name: no-empty-alternative
rules.notes: Rust pattern scanner port of eslint-plugin-regexp/no-empty-alternative for leading, trailing, and adjacent empty alternatives.

rules.name: no-zero-quantifier
rules.notes: Rust pattern scanner port of eslint-plugin-regexp/no-zero-quantifier for {0} and {0,0} quantifiers.

rules.name: no-octal
rules.notes: Rust pattern scanner port of eslint-plugin-regexp/no-octal for octal escape sequences beginning with \0.

rules.name: no-control-character
rules.notes: Rust pattern scanner port of eslint-plugin-regexp/no-control-character for raw, escaped, or constructor-decoded control characters.

rules.name: sort-flags
rules.notes: Rust/Oxc flag scanner port of eslint-plugin-regexp/sort-flags for RegExp literals and static RegExp constructors.

rules.name: require-unicode-regexp
rules.notes: Rust/Oxc flag scanner port of eslint-plugin-regexp/require-unicode-regexp for regexps missing u or v flags.

rules.name: confusing-quantifier
rules.notes: Pattern walker addition: at `*` and `?` peek next byte for `?` (lazy modifier); for braced quantifiers reuse parse_brace_quantifier and check the Star/Question shapes when followed by `?`. `{0,5}?` and other braced forms outside the parser are intentionally deferred.

rules.name: control-character-escape
rules.notes: Pattern-level scan via first_literal_control_character: walks the pattern chars and reports the first raw character with code point < 0x20 or 0x7F (i.e. an actual control byte). Unlike no-control-character, this helper does NOT decode escape sequences — `\xHH` reaching the regex engine as four characters is not flagged, only literal control bytes that landed in the pattern via a host-language escape like `\u0001` in a JS string literal.

rules.name: grapheme-string-literal
rules.notes: Class-level scan via class_has_useless_string_literal: walks the v-mode `[...]` body for `\q{X}` literals whose body is exactly one ASCII alphanumeric character; that wraps a single bare char to no effect. Empty literals (`\q{}`) stay with no-empty-string-literal; multi-character literals are deferred (they need grapheme analysis).

rules.name: hexadecimal-escape
rules.notes: Default `never` config: pattern-level scan via first_hex_x_escape flags `\xHH` and recommends the `\u{HH}` equivalent. Other escapes are skipped through the existing skip_escape walker.

rules.name: letter-case
rules.notes: Default `letter-case` configuration: flags any `\xHH`, `\uHHHH`, or `\u{H+}` escape whose hex digits contain an uppercase letter; the diagnostic carries the original escape and its lowercase replacement. Pattern-level scan; Vitest tests cover all three escape shapes and pure-decimal escapes (which stay valid).

rules.name: match-any
rules.notes: Detects `[\s\S]`, `[\d\D]`, `[\w\W]` character classes (in either order) as character classes that match every character; negated classes and asymmetric pairs are ignored. Rust core; Vitest plugin tests cover anti-pairs across families.

rules.name: negation
rules.notes: Class-level scan via `class_negated_shorthand_letter` flags `[^\X]` where the body is exactly one predefined character-class shorthand (d/D/s/S/w/W); each case has a single unambiguous shorthand replacement (`\D`/`\d`/`\S`/`\s`/`\W`/`\w`). Multi-element bodies, ranges, literal letters, and bodies needing v-mode set semantics are intentionally deferred.

rules.name: no-contradiction-with-assertion
rules.notes: Narrow Rust pattern scan via has_assertion_contradiction for a word-boundary followed by a same-class min-zero quantifier; covered by Rust unit tests and native/plugin Vitest tests.

rules.name: no-dupe-characters-character-class
rules.notes: Class-level scan via class_first_duplicate_literal: walks the class body with a 128-bit on-stack bitmap; skips escapes and ranges so it never false-positives on `\\d\\d` or `a-c`. Reports the first ASCII literal that appears twice.

rules.name: no-dupe-disjunctions
rules.notes: Builds on the per-alt prev_alt_first_byte tracker. At each | and at close ), when the current alternative is single ASCII alphanumeric AND equals the previously recorded alt byte, the duplicate is flagged. Multi-byte alts, escape alts, and class alts opt out (all_alts_single_literal becomes false). Capturing groups not in scope.

rules.name: no-empty-lookarounds-assertion
rules.notes: GroupPrefix gains is_lookaround so the existing pattern walker can flag any of `(?=)` / `(?!)` / `(?<=)` / `(?<!)` whose body is empty; non-capturing empty groups stay with no-empty-group.

rules.name: no-empty-string-literal
rules.notes: Substring scan via pattern_has_empty_string_literal: the `\q{}` syntax is only legal in `v`-flag patterns, so seeing the literal sequence implies v-mode without needing a flag check.

rules.name: no-escape-backspace
rules.notes: Detected at the literal level: `\b` is flagged whenever it appears inside a `[...]` character class. Rust core via pattern walker; covered by insta-style unit tests and Vitest plugin tests.

rules.name: no-extra-lookaround-assertions
rules.notes: Same atom-count tracker as no-trivially-nested-assertion, but targets lookaround wrappers: at close of a `(?=` / `(?!` / `(?<=` / `(?<!` group whose body is exactly one nested lookaround atom, the outer assertion is reported as redundant.

rules.name: no-invisible-character
rules.notes: Pattern-level scan via first_invisible_character: flags literal occurrences of NBSP, ZWSP/ZWNJ/ZWJ, BOM, narrow/medium/ideographic spaces, U+2028/U+2029, and friends. Escaped hex sequences are intentionally skipped. Vitest covers NBSP, ZWSP, and escaped-hex (must not fire).

rules.name: no-lazy-ends
rules.notes: Pattern-level scan via pattern_ends_with_lazy_quantifier: walks the pattern from the right end to detect `*?`, `+?`, `??`, or `{n,m}?` at the very end. Escaped quantifier characters (`\*?`) are excluded via a backslash-parity check; patterns ending in `$` or other anchors after the lazy quantifier are not flagged.

rules.name: no-legacy-features
rules.notes: JS-side check at `StaticMemberExpression` for the legacy `RegExp.$1`-`RegExp.$9`, `RegExp.input`, `RegExp.$_`, `RegExp.lastMatch`, `RegExp.lastParen`, `RegExp.leftContext`, `RegExp.rightContext` static properties; deprecated `RegExp.prototype.compile` is deferred to a future type-aware port. Rust core; Vitest tests cover each property and unrelated member access.

rules.name: no-misleading-capturing-group
rules.notes: Narrow form: the canonical already-included shape A<greedy>(A*), where an atom A is greedily quantified (+, *, or {n,}) immediately followed by a capturing group whose body is exactly A* with the byte-identical atom. The preceding greedy quantifier consumes every A so the group always captures empty (removeQuant). Backtracking-ends cases, disjunction-prefixed groups, and non-identical overlapping atoms are deferred to stay valid-sound.

rules.name: no-misleading-unicode-character
rules.notes: Byte-walker port that flags a character-class element or quantifier target only when the engine matches it as MULTIPLE code points: a multi-code-point grapheme placed literally in a class or after a quantifier (combining/variation-selector/ZWJ/regional-indicator/skin-tone sequence, upstream 'Multi'), or a single astral character seen as a surrogate pair in non-`u`/`v` mode (upstream 'Surrogate'). A single code point — a lone ZWJ (U+200D), combining mark, variation selector, lone surrogate, or an astral char under the `u`/`v` flag — never flags, matching upstream's per-grapheme `getProblem`. Only literal code points are inspected; escapes (`\uXXXX`, `\u{...}`), v-mode `\q{...}` string disjunctions, and nested classes are skipped because their raw source is ASCII. Grapheme clustering is approximated with the joiner/modifier ranges that turn several code points into one user-perceived character; when uncertain a code point is its own grapheme, which can only narrow detection.

rules.name: no-missing-g-flag
rules.notes: JS-side CallExpression check on `<expr>.matchAll(...)` and `<expr>.replaceAll(...)` where the first argument is a RegExp literal without the `g` flag. RegExp constructor calls are deferred (need type information).

rules.name: no-non-standard-flag
rules.notes: Flag-level check via first_non_standard_flag: flags any character outside the canonical d/g/i/m/s/u/v/y set. Runs before constructor parse so the diagnostic fires independently of no-invalid-regexp/error. Vitest covers canonical sets and an out-of-set flag.

rules.name: no-obscure-range
rules.notes: Class-level scan via class_first_obscure_range flags `X-Y` ranges whose endpoints cross ASCII category boundaries (digit / uppercase / lowercase / other). Escaped endpoints are skipped because their equivalence is hard to judge without decoding. The check stays sound (no false positives on within-category ranges).

rules.name: no-optional-assertion
rules.notes: GroupPrefix gains is_lookaround so the closing paren handler can peek the next byte: if a lookaround is followed by `?`, it is reported. Other quantifiers on assertions are syntax errors in the regexp parser anyway, so we only target the `?` case explicitly.

rules.name: no-potentially-useless-backreference
rules.notes: Rust pattern state tracks capture groups directly quantified with `?` or `*` and flags backreferences to those maybe-unmatched groups; covered by Rust unit tests and native/plugin Vitest tests.

rules.name: no-standalone-backslash
rules.notes: Narrow form: scans for `\c[non-letter]` sequences (the control-character escape `\cX` requires an ASCII letter after `\c`; any other character, or end-of-pattern, is a standalone backslash). Only fires without the `u`/`v` flag (with those flags the pattern is already a parse error caught by no-invalid-regexp).

rules.name: no-super-linear-backtracking
rules.notes: Narrow form: the canonical nested-unbounded-quantifier shape `(<atom><unbounded>)<unbounded>` (e.g. `(?:a+)+`, `(a*)*`), where the group body is exactly one atom with an unbounded greedy quantifier and the group is itself unbounded-quantified. The inner quantifier can reach itself through the outer loop, causing exponential backtracking (self). Anchored bodies like `(a+b)+`, bounded quantifiers, and unquantified outers/inners are excluded to stay valid-sound.

rules.name: no-super-linear-move
rules.notes: Narrow form: a leading unbounded greedy quantifier on the first atom (offset 0, so any `^`/`\b`/lookbehind start anchor precludes it) followed by a required rejecting suffix whose first atom is not itself optional (`?`/`*`). Such a pattern scans in quadratic time because the quantifier is retried from every start position. The sticky (`y`) flag is excluded to match the upstream default ignoreSticky:true. `^`-under-m, leading-assertion, and partial-usage cases are deferred to stay valid-sound.

rules.name: no-trivially-nested-assertion
rules.notes: GroupState gains per-alternative atom_count and last_atom_was_lookaround. At close `)`, when a non-capturing group has not seen `|`, its body is exactly one atom, AND that atom was a lookaround close, the wrapper is reported as trivially nested.

rules.name: no-trivially-nested-quantifier
rules.notes: Reuses GroupState.body_start tracker plus a narrow byte-shape check at close: when a non-capturing group has body of exactly two bytes (ASCII alphanumeric + */+/?) AND the byte after the close paren is itself a quantifier, the wrapper is redundant. Multi-byte bodies, escapes, classes, and braced inner quantifiers are deferred.

rules.name: no-unused-capturing-group
rules.notes: Narrow form: the only fully-sound literal-only case is a capturing group inside a regex literal used directly as `/(...)/.test(x)`. RegExp#test returns only a boolean, so its capturing groups are provably unused. pattern_has_capturing_group_and_no_backreference requires at least one capturing group and no in-pattern backreference (\1 / \k<name>). Variable-held literals, .search/.match/.replace data-flow cases are deferred to stay valid-sound.

rules.name: no-useless-assertions
rules.notes: Narrow Rust word-boundary scan via has_useless_word_boundary for `\b`/`\B` between literal neighbours with known word class; covered by Rust unit tests and native/plugin Vitest tests.

rules.name: no-useless-backreference
rules.notes: Narrow byte-walker port that tracks `capture_count` while scanning a literal pattern and flags any `\N` escape where N (single digit 1-9) is greater than the number of capturing groups opened so far. This covers forward references (`\1(a)`) and out-of-range references (`(a)\2`). Multi-digit references, named backreferences, and references inside lookarounds or non-traversed alternatives are intentionally deferred.

rules.name: no-useless-character-class
rules.notes: Class-level scan via class_is_useless_single_literal flags `[X]` where the body is exactly one ASCII literal with no regex meaning of its own; negation, escapes, ranges, and multi-element classes are deferred to keep the check sound (no false positives).

rules.name: no-useless-dollar-replacements
rules.notes: JS-side CallExpression check on `<expr>.replace(<regex>, <string>)` / `replaceAll`. Scans the literal replacement string for a `$0` token (skipping `$$`); `$0` is never a valid backreference because capture groups start at 1, so the dollar-zero is always interpreted as a literal `$0` and almost always a typo. Dynamic replacement arguments are deferred.

rules.name: no-useless-escape
rules.notes: Pattern-level scan via first_useless_escape: walks the pattern bytes, skips any [...] class via find_class_end, and reports the first \X where X is in a curated list of punctuation chars that have no special meaning at top level (colon/semicolon/comma/equals/bang/hash/at/lt/gt/ampersand/underscore/percent/tilde/quote/dquote/slash). Known escape sequences and escapes inside classes are deferred.

rules.name: no-useless-flag
rules.notes: PatternAnalysis gains has_unescaped_dot and has_unescaped_anchor (set by the existing walker on `.` / `^` / `$` outside character classes). After scanning, the rule consults the flags string: `s` without `.` is inert, and `m` without `^`/`$` is inert. Other flags (`i`, etc.) are deferred — they need richer letter/case analysis.

rules.name: no-useless-lazy
rules.notes: Narrow byte-walker port that detects fixed-count brace quantifiers `{n}` and `{n,n}` followed by a lazy `?` modifier. On a fixed-count quantifier the lazy flag is a no-op because the engine always matches exactly `n` repetitions. Other useless-lazy shapes (lazy quantifier whose body matches the empty string, or lazy quantifier followed by an assertion that is already satisfied) need a real regex AST and are intentionally deferred.

rules.name: no-useless-non-capturing-group
rules.notes: GroupPrefix gains is_non_capturing, GroupState tracks body_start. At close `)`, when the group is non-capturing, has no `|` seen, and contains exactly one ASCII alphanumeric byte (and the following byte is not a quantifier), the wrapper is reported as useless. Multi-byte bodies, escapes, classes, nested groups, and quantified groups are intentionally deferred.

rules.name: no-useless-quantifier
rules.notes: Pattern-level scan via first_useless_one_quantifier: walks the pattern bytes, skips classes via find_class_end, and reports the first literal `{1}` or `{1,1}` at top level. Other quantifiers with min=max=1 forms beyond these two are deferred.

rules.name: no-useless-range
rules.notes: Class-level scan via class_has_useless_range flags `X-X` ranges whose start and end are the same literal ASCII character; escaped or compound ranges (`\d-\d`, `\xHH-\xHH`) are intentionally deferred.

rules.name: no-useless-set-operand
rules.notes: Narrow Rust v-mode set-operation scan via has_useless_set_operand for redundant shorthand intersections and subtractions; covered by Rust unit tests and native/plugin Vitest tests.

rules.name: no-useless-string-literal
rules.notes: Parallel emission with grapheme-string-literal: both fire on `\q{X}` with a single ASCII alphanumeric body. Users can enable them independently. Empty / multi-character bodies are deferred.

rules.name: no-useless-two-nums-quantifier
rules.notes: Detects `{n,n}` with `n >= 1` and reports it as rewritable to `{n}` (the `n == 0` case stays with `no-zero-quantifier`). Pattern walker only; covered by Rust unit tests and Vitest plugin tests.

rules.name: optimal-lookaround-quantifier
rules.notes: Reuses GroupState.body_start. At close of a lookaround group, when no | has been seen and the body is exactly 2 bytes (ASCII alphanumeric + * or ?), the inner quantifier accepts the empty match so the assertion always succeeds. + (one-or-more) is excluded; multi-byte bodies and escapes are deferred.

rules.name: optimal-quantifier-concatenation
rules.notes: Narrow Rust pattern scan via has_mergeable_quantifier_concatenation for adjacent same-element quantifiers where at least one side is greedily unbounded; covered by Rust unit tests and native/plugin Vitest tests.

rules.name: prefer-character-class
rules.notes: GroupState tracks current_alt_start (byte offset of the alternative being parsed) and all_alts_single_literal (a running bool). At each | and at close ), the current alternative is inspected: alt_len must be 1 AND that byte must be ASCII alphanumeric. When the non-capturing group has seen at least one | and the flag is still true, prefer-character-class fires. Escapes, classes, groups, and multi-byte alts are deferred.

rules.name: prefer-d
rules.notes: Detects character classes that are exactly `[0-9]` or `[^0-9]` via class_is_digit_range; the diagnostic data carries both the original class and the `\d`/`\D` replacement. Pattern walker only; Vitest plugin tests cover the canonical, negated, subset, and extra-element forms.

rules.name: prefer-escape-replacement-dollar-char
rules.notes: JS-side CallExpression check on <expr>.replace / replaceAll. Scans the literal replacement string and reports the first '$' that is NOT followed by a valid replacement-reference char (digit, '&', "'", '`', '<', '$'). The '$$' escape is the canonical form for a literal dollar.

rules.name: prefer-lookaround
rules.notes: Narrow form (both-ends case only): `.replace`/`.replaceAll` of a non-g regex literal `(B1)MID(B2)` whose string replacement starts with the group-1 ref and ends with the group-2 ref (each used exactly once). prefer_lookaround_groups requires plain fixed-length group bodies, a non-empty plain middle, exactly two groups, and differing boundary bytes so the lookaround conversion `(?<=B1)MID(?=B2)` is provably safe. The g flag, adjacent/empty middles, overlapping boundaries, quantifier/alternation bodies, reused refs, and dynamic replacements are all excluded to stay valid-sound against the overlap-heavy upstream valid suite.

rules.name: prefer-named-backreference
rules.notes: Pattern-level scan via first_numbered_backreference_with_named_group: gates on the presence of `(?<` (named capture) anywhere in the pattern, then walks for `\N` (N in 1-9) outside classes. Classes are skipped because `\1` is literal there.

rules.name: prefer-named-capture-group
rules.notes: Detects any anonymous capturing group `(...)` at literal level via the existing PatternAnalysis walker; named captures and non-capturing groups are ignored. Rust core; Vitest plugin tests cover valid and invalid cases.

rules.name: prefer-named-replacement
rules.notes: JS-side CallExpression check for `<expr>.replace(<regexp literal>, <string literal>)` and `replaceAll`: fire when the regex source contains a named capture marker `(?<` and the replacement string contains `$N` (1-9). `$$` is escaped and skipped. Dynamic arguments are deferred.

rules.name: prefer-plus-quantifier
rules.notes: Detects `{1,}` and reports it as rewritable to `+`. Pattern walker only (no full regexp parse); covered by Rust unit tests and Vitest plugin tests.

rules.name: prefer-predefined-assertion
rules.notes: Reuses GroupState.body_start. At close of a lookaround group, when no | has been seen and the body is exactly one byte equal to ^ or $, the lookaround is reported as redundant: the bare anchor is equivalent. Empty lookarounds remain no-empty-lookarounds-assertion territory. Multi-byte bodies and non-anchor single-byte bodies are deferred.

rules.name: prefer-quantifier
rules.notes: Built on the GroupState body-byte tracker introduced for no-useless-non-capturing-group. At close `)`, when the group is non-capturing, has not seen `|`, the body is exactly one ASCII alphanumeric byte, AND the byte after `)` is a quantifier (`*`, `+`, `?`, or `{`), the wrapper is reported. The quantifier could apply directly to the bare atom. Multi-atom bodies and escape/class/group bodies are deferred.

rules.name: prefer-question-quantifier
rules.notes: Detects `{0,1}` and reports it as rewritable to `?`. Pattern walker only; covered by Rust unit tests and Vitest plugin tests.

rules.name: prefer-range
rules.notes: Class-level scan via class_first_collapsible_run finds three or more consecutive ASCII letters/digits at the top level of a class and reports them with the equivalent `a-c`-style replacement. Escapes and existing ranges are skipped to keep the check conservative.

rules.name: prefer-regexp-exec
rules.notes: JS-side CallExpression check on `<expr>.match(<regexp literal>)` where the literal has no `g` flag. RegExp constructor calls are deferred because deciding whether the receiver is a string needs type information.

rules.name: prefer-regexp-test
rules.notes: Narrow-form port: flags `.exec()` calls in boolean context when the receiver is not a known string, and `.match(<regexp literal>)` calls in boolean context when the receiver is a known string and the argument has no `g` flag. Boolean contexts detected: if/while/do-while/for tests, `!`, `Boolean()`, `&&`/`||`, ternary test, `=== null` / `!== null` comparisons.

rules.name: prefer-result-array-groups
rules.notes: Narrow form: flags `arr[N]` numeric access (N>=1, integer literal) when N maps to a named capturing group of the backing regex literal. Sources resolved one hop via scoping: inline `<regexLiteral>.exec(x)[N]`, `var arr = <regexLiteral>.exec(x)`, or `var arr = <knownString>.match(<regexLiteral>)` (non-g). Reuses named_group_mask. Unnamed groups, index 0, g-flag match, unknown receivers, and `.groups` access are never flagged.

rules.name: prefer-set-operation
rules.notes: Narrow Rust v-mode scan via has_preferable_set_operation for a character lookaround adjacent to a compatible character element; covered by Rust unit tests and native/plugin Vitest tests.

rules.name: prefer-star-quantifier
rules.notes: Detects `{0,}` and reports it as rewritable to `*`. Pattern walker only; covered by Rust unit tests and Vitest plugin tests.

rules.name: prefer-unicode-codepoint-escapes
rules.notes: Pattern-level scan via first_surrogate_pair_escape detects `\uHHHH\uHHHH` pairs where the first hex is a high surrogate (D800-DBFF) and the second a low surrogate (DC00-DFFF). The diagnostic carries the original pair and the `\u{H+}` codepoint replacement.

rules.name: prefer-w
rules.notes: Detects character classes that are exactly the four word-char elements (`a-z`, `A-Z`, `0-9`, `_`) in any order via class_is_word_char_set; covers both positive and negated forms. Pattern walker only; Vitest tests cover reordered, partial, and superset variants.

rules.name: require-unicode-sets-regexp
rules.notes: Flag-level check that mirrors require-unicode-regexp but specifically requires the `v` (unicode sets) flag rather than `u` or `v`. Patterns that only carry `u` still fire this rule when it is enabled.

rules.name: simplify-set-operations
rules.notes: Narrow Rust v-mode set-operation scan via has_simplifiable_set_operation for intersections with negated nested-class operands; covered by Rust unit tests and native/plugin Vitest tests.

rules.name: sort-alternatives
rules.notes: GroupState tracks prev_alt_first_byte and alts_in_order alongside current_alt_start. At each | (and at close `)`), when the alternative is exactly one ASCII alphanumeric byte, its byte is compared with the previously recorded alt; if the new byte is less than the previous, alts_in_order = false. The rule fires only when seen_pipe is true AND all alts were single literals AND ordering was violated. Escapes, classes, nested groups, and multi-byte alts opt out (all_alts_single_literal becomes false).

rules.name: sort-character-class-elements
rules.notes: Class-level scan via class_has_unsorted_literal_elements: walks the class body once, rejecting on any escape, range, negation, or non-alphanumeric byte. Reports when the remaining all-literal elements are not in ascending byte order. Sorting semantics across escapes/ranges are intentionally deferred.

rules.name: strict
rules.notes: Narrow Rust strict-mode scan via first_strict_violation for non-u/non-v regexps with invalid escapes, unescaped source characters, and quantified assertions; covered by Rust unit tests and native/plugin Vitest tests.

rules.name: unicode-escape
rules.notes: Default `unicodeCodePointEscape` config: pattern-level scan via first_fixed_unicode_escape flags the fixed-width `\uHHHH` form and recommends `\u{HHHH}`; the `\u{H+}` form is left alone.

rules.name: unicode-property
rules.notes: Narrow form: under the upstream default config (generalCategory: 'never') an explicit General_Category key in a \p{gc=...} / \p{General_Category=...} property escape is redundant. has_unnecessary_general_category_key flags only that key-value form with an exact 'gc' or 'General_Category' key; keyless properties, Script keys, and binary properties are never flagged.

rules.name: use-ignore-case
rules.notes: Class-level scan via class_has_case_pair: 26x2 on-stack ASCII bitmaps collect lower/upper letter occurrences within each character class. Reports when any letter has both forms present in the same class AND the regex flags string does not contain `i`. Ranges and escapes are skipped to keep the check sound.

Flags a value-consuming array method (map/filter/every/some/find/findIndex/findLast/findLastIndex/reduce/reduceRight/flatMap/sort) whose first-argument inline function/arrow callback never returns a value. Conservative any-return semantics: flagged only when the block body contains zero value-returning return statements (a bare return; does not count); arrow expression bodies imply a return and are never flagged. Body walk descends through control flow but stops at nested functions. Type-free: keys off method name on any receiver (rare non-array over-report accepted). forEach and Array.from are not covered. No upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages were consulted or copied.

Gating on the Volume construct name keeps this distinct from aws-efs-unencrypted (which also keys off encrypted: false). The implicit/absent form (no encrypted property), encrypted: true, non-Volume callees, and encrypted: false outside a new Volume(...) are deliberately NOT flagged to stay zero-false-positive. SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from public RSPEC description only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

Gating on the FileSystem construct name keeps this distinct from the EBS-volume rule, which also keys off encrypted: false. Encryption enabled (encrypted: true), the encrypted property absent (out of scope), other constructs (new Volume(...)), and calls without an options object are deliberately NOT flagged. SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from public RSPEC description only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: aws-restricted-ip-admin-access

Flags an AWS CDK security-group ingress rule that opens an administration port to all IP addresses: a call whose callee is a static member expression named addIngressRule, with at least two arguments, where argument 0 (the peer) is a call whose terminal callee name is anyIpv4 or anyIpv6 (Peer.anyIpv4()/ec2.Peer.anyIpv4()) AND argument 1 (the port) is a call whose terminal callee name is tcp (Port.tcp(...)) whose first argument is the numeric literal 22 (SSH) or 3389 (RDP). Requiring both the any-IP peer and an admin Port.tcp together is distinctive to this insecure pattern, keeping it zero-false-positive. Specific CIDR peers, non-admin ports (e.g. Port.tcp(443), Port.allTraffic()), fewer than two arguments and non-addIngressRule callees are not flagged. The Cfn-level form (cidrIp "0.0.0.0/0" with fromPort/toPort), connections.allowFrom and Port.tcpRange variants are intentionally not detected to stay zero-false-positive, so this port deliberately under-reports. rules.name: confidential-information-logging
rules.notes: Clean-room port of S5757. Flags a Signale logger configured without secret masking: a new-expression whose callee (after get_inner_expression) is Signale (bare identifier or static member ending in .Signale) whose first argument is an object literal with a "secrets" property whose value is an empty array literal ([]). The Signale class paired with an empty secrets list is distinctive to the signale logging API, so this exact shape stays zero-false-positive. Non-empty secrets (secrets: ["password"]), a missing secrets key (new Signale({})), non-array secrets values, and non-Signale callees are deliberately NOT flagged. SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from public RSPEC description only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: cyclomatic-complexity
rules.notes: Clean-room port of S1541 (per-function McCabe cyclomatic complexity). Base complexity 1 per function; +1 for each if (including else-if), for, for-in, for-of, while, do-while, case clause (not default), catch, ternary (?:), and logical expression (&&/||/??). Top-level decision points outside any function are ignored. Configurable threshold option (default 10). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; implemented from public RSPEC only.

rules.name: declarations-in-global-scope
rules.notes: Clean-room port of S3798. Reports named top-level function declarations and module-scope var declarations, including var declarations nested in top-level blocks; allows let/const, anonymous default function exports, var require(...) bindings, declarations inside functions, and class static block locals. Behaviour was implemented from observed output and public RSPEC only.

rules.name: deprecation
rules.notes: Clean-room port (S1874). Narrow same-file subset: flags identifier references to local function/class declarations immediately preceded by a block comment containing @deprecated. Cross-module references and variable declarations excluded to avoid false positives in the absence of type information. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: destructuring-assignment-syntax
rules.notes: Clean-room port (S3514). Flags the second (and subsequent) consecutive single-declarator const/let declarations that each extract a matching-named property from the same plain identifier (e.g. const a = obj.a; const b = obj.b; is flagged at the second declaration). Conservative zero-false-positive design: only static member access with matching binding and property names on a plain (non-chained) identifier source qualifies. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: different-types-comparison

rules.name: disabled-auto-escaping
rules.notes: Clean-room port of S5247. Flags disabling a template engine's HTML auto-escaping via two distinctive zero-false-positive signals: an object property "noEscape" set to the boolean literal true (Handlebars compile option) and an assignment to the static member Mustache.escape (overriding mustache.js's built-in escaper). The spec's generic markdown-it/marked/kramed signals (html: true, sanitize: false) are deliberately NOT flagged because those key names are too common across unrelated APIs to stay zero-false-positive. SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from public RSPEC description only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: file-permissions
rules.notes: Clean-room port of S2612. Flags fs chmod-family calls (chmod/chmodSync/fchmod/fchmodSync/lchmod/lchmodSync) whose numeric-literal mode sets any "others" bit (mode & 0o007 != 0), and umask calls whose numeric-literal value fails to restrict all "others" bits (value & 0o007 != 0o007). Only numeric-literal modes are checked (zero-false-positive). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from public RSPEC description only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: file-uploads
rules.notes: Clean-room port of S2598. Flags a multer diskStorage configuration that omits an explicit destination: a call whose callee is a static member expression named diskStorage whose object-literal first argument has no "destination" property (identifier or string key). Without a destination, uploaded files default to the OS temporary directory. The diskStorage name is distinctive, so matching on it regardless of receiver stays zero-false-positive; diskStorage calls without an object argument are skipped. Formidable's uploadDir hotspot requires instance dataflow and is out of scope. SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from public RSPEC description only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: fixme-tag
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S1134). Case-sensitive substring match for all-caps FIXME in comment text. Lowercase/mixed-case variants (fixme, FixMe) are intentionally excluded — case-insensitive matching would require allocation in the Rust core and is a follow-up. One diagnostic per comment containing FIXME, at the comment span.

rules.name: for-in
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S1535). Structural-only check: reports a for...in loop whose body is not a single if statement (directly or as the sole statement in a block). Does not inspect the if condition. No upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages consulted or copied.

rules.name: function-inside-loop
rules.notes: Clean-room port of Sonar key S1515. Flags any function declaration, function expression, or arrow function created directly inside a loop body (for/for-in/for-of/while/do-while) relative to the nearest enclosing function; a function nested inside another function resets the loop context. Immediately invoked function expressions are exempt. No closure/capture analysis is performed (flags every eligible function in a loop, like upstream). Reported at the function span. Implemented via a per-function-scope loop-depth stack. Message and tests independently authored.

rules.name: function-name
rules.notes: Clean-room Rust/Oxc port of Sonar key S100. Checks function declarations, function/arrow variable initializers, class/object method keys, and object property keys whose value is a function/arrow against the configured format (default ^[_a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*$). Ignores assignments, IIFEs, private names, and string/numeric keys to match observed upstream behavior. Includes native API, plugin harness, option-forwarding, and CLI coverage.

rules.name: generator-without-yield
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S3531). Uses a SmallVec<[bool; 8]> generator frame stack in the Oxc visitor: a frame is pushed on entry to each generator function with a body and marked true if any yield is visited; the frame is popped on exit and the generator is reported when the frame is still false. Yield correctly attributes to the nearest enclosing generator because yield cannot cross a non-generator function boundary (SyntaxError). Async generators (async function*) are included. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: index-of-compare-to-positive-number
rules.notes: Clean-room port of Sonar key S2692. Purely syntactic: flags BinaryExpression where one side is a CallExpression whose callee is a StaticMemberExpression with property name 'indexOf' or 'lastIndexOf', and the other side is a numeric literal, with operator/value excluding index 0: indexOf > N (N>=0), indexOf >= N (N>=1), and mirrored forms N < indexOf (N>=0), N <= indexOf (N>=1). Compliant forms >= 0, > -1, === -1, !== -1 are not flagged. No type info required. Message and tests independently authored from RSPEC S2692 public documentation.

rules.name: insecure-cookie
rules.notes: Clean-room port of S2092. Flags an object literal that configures a cookie with the secure flag disabled: an ObjectExpression containing a property whose key (static identifier or string literal) is "secure" with the boolean literal value false, gated to stay zero-false-positive by requiring that the same object literal also contains at least one distinctive cookie-marker sibling key (httpOnly, sameSite, maxAge, domain, path, or signed). The bare key "secure" is too generic (TLS sockets, database drivers, and HTTP clients use it too), so a "secure: false" property with no cookie-marker sibling is not flagged; neither is secure: true nor a non-literal secure value. SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from public RSPEC description only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: jsx-no-leaked-render
rules.notes: Clean-room port of S6439. Flags a logical-AND expression that conditionally renders JSX where the left operand is provably numeric: a static member access whose property name is length (the classic `{items.length && <JSX/>}` leak) or a numeric literal, AND whose right operand (after unwrapping parentheses/non-null assertions) is a JSXElement or JSXFragment. A falsy numeric 0 is rendered by React as the text "0" instead of nothing. Boolean comparisons (`> 0`, `!== 0`), Boolean(...), plain identifiers, non-length member accesses, the `||` operator, ternaries and non-JSX right operands are intentionally not flagged to stay zero-false-positive, so this port deliberately under-reports. SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from public RSPEC description only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: label-position
rules.notes: Clean-room port of Sonar key S1439. Reports labels whose direct body is not a loop (for/for-in/for-of/while/do-while) or switch statement; nested labels intentionally report the outer label because it is not directly attached to the breakable statement. Implemented in the Rust scanner with NAPI, API, adapter, and Oxlint CLI coverage.

rules.name: link-with-target-blank

rules.name: max-lines
rules.notes: Clean-room port of S104. Counts physical lines that contain at least one non-whitespace character outside a comment span. Blank lines and lines whose every non-whitespace character is inside a // or /* */ comment are excluded. A single diagnostic is emitted at the start of the file (Span 0,0) when the code-line count strictly exceeds the configurable maximum (default 1000).

rules.name: max-lines-per-function
rules.notes: Clean-room port of S138. Counts code lines (non-blank, non-comment-only) over the function's own line span. Reports FunctionDeclaration, FunctionExpression, and ArrowFunctionExpression when the count strictly exceeds the configurable maximum (default 200). IIFEs (function/arrow that is the direct callee of a CallExpression) are never reported. JSX-containing functions (any function whose subtree contains a JSXElement or JSXFragment) are never reported — this is a deliberate broadening of SonarJS's React-component exclusion; we exclude any JSX-containing function rather than only capitalized-name components, so we never over-report.

rules.name: max-switch-cases
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S1479). Reports a switch statement whose case/default clause count exceeds 30. Threshold is fixed at the SonarJS default of 30 (MAX_CASES); per-rule options infrastructure for configurability is a follow-up task. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: max-union-size
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S4622). Reports a TypeScript union type whose direct member count exceeds 3. Threshold is fixed at the SonarJS default of 3 (MAX_UNION_SIZE); per-rule options infrastructure for configurability is a follow-up task. Each TSUnionType node is counted per-node — nested parenthesized unions are not flattened. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: new-operator-misuse
rules.notes: Clean-room port (S2999). Flags new expressions whose callee is an arrow function directly or via identifier resolution. rules.name: no-all-duplicated-branches

rules.name: no-alphabetical-sort
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S2871). Receivers whose array-ness cannot be proven are conservatively skipped (the upstream rule is type-aware; without TS types we under-report rather than risk false positives on non-array .sort methods). Uses oxc_semantic (built only when this rule or no-misleading-array-reverse is active). No upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages were consulted.

rules.name: no-case-label-in-switch
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S1219). Detects labeled statements appearing directly in the consequent list of a switch case, where they almost certainly represent a mistyped 'case' clause. Only direct children of SwitchCase.consequent are flagged; labels nested inside a block within a case are intentionally out of scope. Overlap with no-labels is intentional — both rules may fire when both are enabled. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-clear-text-protocols
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S5332). Flags string literals containing clear-text URL schemes http://, ftp://, telnet://, ws://, or ldap://. Encrypted schemes and protocol-like labels without // are ignored; dynamic strings and template literals are intentionally out of scope. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-code-after-done
rules.notes: Clean-room port of Sonar key S6079. Flags the first statement that runs after a top-level done() call inside a Mocha test/hook callback (it/test/specify/before/after/beforeEach/afterEach and it/test/specify .only/.skip) whose parameter list declares a plain 'done' identifier. Only the top level of the callback body is inspected (no recursion into nested blocks/functions); a trailing bare 'return;' is exempt. Reported at the offending statement span. Message and tests independently authored.

rules.name: no-collapsible-if
rules.notes: Clean-room port of Sonar key S1066. Reports an outer if statement (at its if keyword) when it has no else clause and its consequent is either a direct inner if or a single-statement block containing an if, and that inner if also has no else clause. The two conditions should be merged with &&. Message and tests independently authored.

rules.name: no-collection-size-mischeck
rules.notes: Clean-room port of Sonar key S3981. Purely syntactic: flags BinaryExpression where one side is a StaticMemberExpression with property name 'length' or 'size' and the other side is numeric literal 0, and the operator is < (always false) or >= (always true), including mirrored forms 0 > x.length and 0 <= x.length. No type info required. Message and tests independently authored.

rules.name: no-commented-code
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S125). Conservative heuristic: extract comment inner text (strip // or /* */), skip JSDoc, TODO/FIXME/NOSONAR/eslint-disable/@ts- tags, and very short content; parse with a fresh oxc_parser Allocator per comment in TSX/module mode; flag only when parsing succeeds with no errors, the program has ≥1 statement, and a strong code signal is present (trimmed text ends with ';', '{', '}', or ',' — OR the single top-level statement is an if/for/while/do-while/for-in/for-of/switch/try/throw/function/class/variable declaration). Prose text reliably fails the code-signal check. SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from public RSPEC description only — no upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages were copied.

rules.name: no-control-regex
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S6324). Flags control characters (U+0000–U+001F) written as \x, \u/\u{}, or \c escapes anywhere in a regex literal including inside character classes. Named escapes \t\n\r\v\f (CharacterKind::SingleEscape) are not flagged. Literal raw control chars and octal escapes are a documented follow-up (false negatives, not false positives). No upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-dead-store
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-dead-store (Sonar key S1854). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-delete-var
rules.notes: Clean-room port of Sonar key S3001. Reports delete applied to a bare variable identifier (delete x), which is a no-op in sloppy mode and a SyntaxError in strict mode. Member-expression operands (delete obj.prop, delete obj[key]) are not flagged. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-duplicate-in-composite
rules.notes: Clean-room port of Sonar key S4621. Reports duplicate type members in TypeScript union or intersection types by comparing member source text. Two-phase borrow approach used (collect spans first, then report). No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-duplicate-string
rules.notes: Clean-room port of Sonar key S1192. Counts qualifying string literal values (>= 10 chars, containing a non-word char, not "application/json") across a file; reports the first occurrence of any value whose count >= threshold (default 3). Import/export sources and JSX attribute values are excluded. Configurable threshold option. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-duplicated-branches
rules.notes: Clean-room port of SonarJS S1871. Rust core checks if/else-if/else chains and switch statements for any two branches with byte-identical implementations (the second duplicate branch is reported). Differs from no-all-duplicated-branches (S3923) which requires ALL branches to match. Empty blocks and fall-through cases are excluded. Head detection for if-chains reuses the shared if_chain_seen set. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages consulted.

rules.name: no-element-overwrite
rules.notes: Clean-room port of SonarJS S4143. Rust core scans consecutive ExpressionStatement pairs in every statement list (program, block, function body, switch case). Flags the first of two adjacent plain-identifier member-expression writes with the same literal key or static property name where the second RHS does not reference the element being written (read-modify-write guard). Only literal computed keys are checked to avoid false positives with variable indices. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages consulted.

rules.name: no-empty-after-reluctant
rules.notes: Clean-room port of S6019 from public RSPEC description only. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were consulted. Flags a reluctant (lazy) quantifier with min==0 when every subsequent term in the same alternative can match the empty string. Conservative can_match_empty: under-reports rather than FPs.

rules.name: no-empty-alternatives
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S6323). Uses the shared regex_ast helper to parse a regex literal and walk every disjunction (top-level + group/lookaround bodies); a disjunction with two or more alternatives where some alternative body is empty is flagged at each empty alternative's span. A single empty alternative with no `|` (an empty group) is left to no-empty-group. Regex-literal scope only; the new RegExp(string) form is a documented follow-up. No upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-empty-character-class
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S2639). Detects regex literals containing an empty character class [] that can never match. Scope: regex literals only; new RegExp("[]") string-argument form is out of scope (follow-up). Uses visit_reg_exp_literal hook; pattern text via lit.regex.pattern.text.as_str().

rules.name: no-empty-collection
rules.notes: Clean-room port of SonarJS S4158 (inverse of no-unused-collection). Rust/Oxc semantic analysis tracks const/let bindings initialised with an EMPTY array-like collection ([] or new Map/Set/Array/WeakMap/WeakSet with no args). Object literals are excluded to stay zero-FP. Every resolved reference is classified as write/populate (populating method call push/unshift/splice/set/add/fill/copyWithin, or plain = member assignment), read (curated query/iteration members, for..of, spread, index read), or ambiguous (anything else). The declaration is flagged only when at least one read exists and zero writes and zero ambiguous references do. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages consulted.

rules.name: no-empty-group
rules.notes: Clean-room port (SonarJS key S6331). Detects empty capturing and non-capturing groups in regex literals via oxc_regular_expression AST. Never copied upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-empty-test-file
rules.notes: Clean-room port (SonarJS key S2187). Flags test files (base name contains .test. or .spec.) that contain no it() or test() call expressions; describe() alone does not count. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-equals-in-for-termination
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S888). Reports an equality operator (==, !=, ===, !==) in a for-loop termination condition only when the counter operand is advanced by a recognised non-unit additive step (i += k / i -= k / i = i ± k with k != 1). Unit steps (++, --, ± 1) are exempt per the CERT MSC21 / S888 exception. Counters with no header update, an update on a different variable, or an opaque update are not flagged, favouring fewer false positives. No upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-exclusive-tests
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S6426). Covers the .only member-expression form on the six known test-runner identifiers: describe, it, test, context, suite, specify. Jasmine fdescribe/fit and other focused-test prefix forms are out of scope and noted as a follow-up.

rules.name: no-extra-arguments
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S930). Conservative scope: only const/let/var-assigned FunctionExpression and ArrowFunctionExpression callees resolved via semantic analysis. Zero-FP guards: rest parameters, spread arguments, and functions whose source text contains 'arguments'. Function declarations are a follow-up. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-fallthrough
rules.notes: Clean-room Rust/Oxc port of SonarJS no-fallthrough (S128). Reports non-empty switch cases that can continue into the next case, with support for break/return/throw/continue termination, all-branches-terminating if/try forms, empty grouped cases, and intentional fallthrough comments. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-for-in-iterable
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S4139). Flags a for...in loop whose iterated value is provably an array: a direct array literal (for (i in [1,2,3])) or an identifier resolving via semantic analysis to a never-reassigned array-literal binding (const a=[1,2,3]; for (i in a)). Reported at the ForInStatement span. The upstream rule is type-aware and also flags strings, typed arrays, and other iterables; without TS type information this port conservatively flags arrays only and under-reports rather than risk false positives (known parity risk). Uses oxc_semantic (built only when this rule, no-misleading-array-reverse, or no-alphabetical-sort is active). No upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages were consulted.

rules.name: no-function-declaration-in-block
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S1530). Flags a FunctionDeclaration that is a direct statement child of a BlockStatement (if/loop/try bodies, bare blocks); reported via check_no_function_declaration_in_block from visit_block_statement. Function bodies are FunctionBody nodes (not BlockStatement) so top-of-function declarations are not flagged; ambient `declare function` is skipped. Narrow form: function declarations directly in a switch-case body are a documented follow-up. No upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-global-this
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S2990). Flags ThisExpression when this_binding_depth==0 (global this). Increments depth on enter of regular functions (visit_function), class field initializers (visit_property_definition), class accessor properties (visit_accessor_property), and class static blocks (visit_static_block). Arrow functions do NOT increment depth. Computed property key context (this in [this.x] = 1) is treated conservatively as non-global (not flagged) — parity risk noted. Super/decorator contexts are not specially handled.

rules.name: no-globals-shadowing
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-globals-shadowing (Sonar key S2137). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-gratuitous-expressions
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-gratuitous-expressions (Sonar key S2589). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-hardcoded-ip
rules.notes: Clean-room port from RSPEC S1313. Scans string literals for embedded IPv4/IPv6 addresses via substring search with std::net parse validation. Excludes loopback (127.0.0.0/8), unspecified (0.0.0.0), broadcast (255.255.255.255), IPv6 loopback (::1), IPv6 unspecified (::), documentation range (2001:db8::/32), and IPv4-mapped loopback (::ffff:127.x.x.x). No options.

rules.name: no-hardcoded-passwords
rules.notes: Clean-room port from public RSPEC S2068. Detects hardcoded string literals assigned to password-named identifiers (VariableDeclarator, ObjectProperty, AssignmentExpression). Credential-word set: password/passwd/pwd/passphrase. Value guards: empty/short/placeholder-equal-to-name/well-known-placeholders. No semantic analysis needed.

rules.name: no-hardcoded-secrets
rules.notes: Clean-room port from public RSPEC S6418. Sibling of no-hardcoded-passwords: detects hardcoded string literals assigned to secret-named identifiers (VariableDeclarator, ObjectProperty, AssignmentExpression). Credential-word set (whole-name, case-insensitive): secret/secrets/apikey/api_key/apiKey/token/auth_token/authtoken/accesstoken/access_token/credential/credentials/privatekey/private_key. Value guards: empty/short/placeholder-equal-to-name/well-known-placeholders (token, secret, changeit, xxx, undefined, null, none, your-api-key, password, ***, todo). No semantic analysis needed.

rules.name: no-hook-setter-in-body
rules.notes: Clean-room port of S6442. Flags a React useState setter called directly in a component body. Function-local detection scoped to one function/arrow body: pass 1 records setter names from a VariableDeclarator whose init is a call to useState or React.useState and whose id is an array pattern with at least two elements where the second is a binding identifier; pass 2 flags a direct top-level ExpressionStatement whose expression is a call to a plain identifier matching a recorded setter. Only direct statements of the same body are inspected, so setter calls nested in inner functions/arrows (event handlers, useEffect callbacks, .map), conditionals, loops, try, or JSX are not flagged. Setters are recorded per body, so a setter from one component cannot flag a call in another. No semantic analysis needed. SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from public RSPEC description only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-identical-conditions
rules.notes: Clean-room port of SonarJS S1862. Rust core uses chain-head detection via if_chain_seen to process each if/else-if chain exactly once; conditions compared by source text; duplicate later conditions reported. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages consulted.

rules.name: no-identical-expressions
rules.notes: Clean-room port of SonarJS S1764. Rust core checks BinaryExpression (comparison, bitwise, arithmetic operators) and LogicalExpression (&&, ||) for textually identical left/right operands; excluded operators (+, *, **, <<, >>, >>>, ??, instanceof, in) are documented in the rule. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages consulted.

rules.name: no-identical-functions
rules.notes: Clean-room port of SonarJS S4144. Reports the second (and further) of two or more functions in the same file whose params+body source text is byte-for-byte identical. Functions under 3 lines are exempt. Expression-bodied arrows are excluded. Comparison is whitespace-sensitive (conservative, under-reports relative to upstream's token-level comparison). No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages consulted.

rules.name: no-ignored-exceptions
rules.notes: Clean-room port of SonarJS S2486. Flags catch clauses whose body block is completely empty and contains no comment. An empty catch with a comment is treated as an intentional silent-ignore and is not flagged. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or message strings were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-ignored-return
rules.notes: Clean-room port (S2201). The upstream is type-aware; this port implements a zero-false-positive narrow subset: only flags calls where the receiver is a string/numeric/array literal, so the type is statically certain without type inference. Literal-receiver narrowing is this port's own design. No upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-implicit-dependencies
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-implicit-dependencies (Sonar key S4328). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-implicit-global
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-implicit-global (Sonar key S2703). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-in-misuse
rules.notes: Clean-room port of SonarJS S4619. Flags 'value in array' expressions where the right operand is a provably-array expression and the left is a non-numeric, non-Array.prototype-member string literal. Conservative zero-false-positive design: only direct array literals or never-reassigned const identifiers resolved via semantic analysis are flagged. Numeric index strings and Array.prototype member names are exempt. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages consulted.

rules.name: no-incomplete-assertions
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-incomplete-assertions (Sonar key S2970). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-inconsistent-returns
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S3801). A per-function/arrow frame stack (return_kind_stack) pushed in visit_function/visit_arrow_function_expression records, per scope, whether an explicit value `return x;` and an explicit bare `return;` were seen (visit_return_statement); on leave the scope is reported when both are present. Returns bind to the nearest scope frame so nested functions are isolated. Narrow form: an explicit value return mixed with an implicit fall-through (no trailing return) is a documented follow-up. No upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-incorrect-string-concat
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-incorrect-string-concat (Sonar key S3402). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-internal-api-use
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-internal-api-use (Sonar key S6627). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-intrusive-permissions
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-intrusive-permissions (Sonar key S5604). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-invalid-regexp
rules.notes: Clean-room port from public RSPEC S5856. Validates string-literal patterns passed to new RegExp(...) and RegExp(...) using the oxc LiteralParser with the cooked string value. Dynamic arguments are skipped to avoid false positives. Dynamic flags fall back to empty-string validation. Invalid flags (unknown letter, duplicate, conflicting u+v) are caught by the flags parser. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-invariant-returns
rules.notes: Clean-room port from public RSPEC S3516. Flags functions where every explicit value return is byte-identical (at least two value returns, no bare return). Pure AST text comparison via self.text(span). No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-inverted-boolean-check
rules.notes: Clean-room port of Sonar key S1940. Reports a logical-NOT applied directly to a comparison BinaryExpression (==, ===, !=, !==, <, <=, >, >=). Logical operators && and || are LogicalExpression nodes and are naturally excluded. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were consulted.

rules.name: no-ip-forward
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-ip-forward (Sonar key S5759). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-labels
rules.notes: Clean-room port of no-labels (Sonar key S1119). Flags every LabeledStatement unconditionally via visit_labeled_statement; reports stmt.label.span. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-literal-call
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-literal-call (Sonar key S6958). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-mime-sniff
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-mime-sniff (Sonar key S5734). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-misleading-array-reverse
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S4043). Flags consuming the return value of the in-place array-mutating methods reverse()/sort() (variable initializer or plain = assignment) when the receiver is provably a reference to a pre-existing array (an identifier resolving via semantic analysis to a never-reassigned binding whose initializer is an array literal). Fresh-array receivers ([...a].reverse(), [1,2].reverse()) and bare side-effect statements are not flagged. Uses oxc_semantic (built only when this rule is active). No upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages were consulted.

rules.name: no-misleading-character-class
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-misleading-character-class (Sonar key S5868). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-mixed-content
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-mixed-content (Sonar key S5730). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-nested-assignment
rules.notes: Clean-room narrow port (Sonar key S1121). Flags an `=` assignment used as an if/while/do-while/for condition (check_no_nested_assignment_condition on the relevant `test`) and the right-hand side of a chained `=` assignment (check_no_nested_assignment_chain from visit_assignment_expression). Restricted to the AssignmentOperator::Assign operator for a zero-false-positive subset; other sub-expression positions (call args, declarator init, literals) and compound operators are a documented follow-up. for-loop init/update are intentionally not flagged. No upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-nested-conditional
rules.notes: Clean-room port of SonarJS S3358. Reports every ConditionalExpression (ternary) that appears nested inside another ConditionalExpression, in any of the outer ternary's three positions (test, consequent, alternate). Implemented via a depth counter in the Oxc AST visitor; each nested ternary is reported at its own span.

rules.name: no-nested-functions
rules.notes: Clean-room port of SonarJS S2004. Reports any function (declaration, expression, or arrow) nested at a depth strictly greater than the configurable threshold (default 4). Implemented via a single u32 depth counter on the Scanner incremented on visit_function/visit_arrow_function_expression entry and decremented on exit. Option name: threshold; NAPI field: noNestedFunctionsThreshold.

rules.name: no-nested-incdec
rules.notes: Clean-room narrow port (Sonar key S881). Flags an UpdateExpression (++/--) used directly as an argument of a CallExpression or NewExpression, matched as Argument::UpdateExpression in check_no_nested_incdec_call/check_no_nested_incdec_new from visit_call_expression/visit_new_expression. Standalone update statements and for-loop update clauses are not flagged. Increments mixed with other operators in arithmetic/index expressions are a documented follow-up. No upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-nested-switch
rules.notes: Clean-room port of upstream rule no-nested-switch (Sonar key S1821). Reports a switch statement nested inside another switch. SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; implemented from observed behavior only — no upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages were copied.

rules.name: no-nested-template-literals
rules.notes: Clean-room port of upstream rule no-nested-template-literals (Sonar key S4624). Reports a template literal nested in another template literal's interpolations. SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; implemented from observed behavior only — no upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages were copied.

rules.name: no-os-command-from-path
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-os-command-from-path (Sonar key S4036). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-parameter-reassignment
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-parameter-reassignment (Sonar key S1226). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-primitive-wrappers
rules.notes: Clean-room port (S1533). Reports NewExpression whose callee is an identifier named Number, String, or Boolean. Rust core uses visit_new_expression + get_inner_expression(); NAPI adapter wires the rule into the plugin. Tests authored independently — no upstream source, fixtures, or messages copied.

rules.name: no-redundant-assignments
rules.notes: Clean-room port of SonarJS S4165. Rust core scans consecutive ExpressionStatement pairs in every statement list (program, block, function body, switch case) for two kinds of redundant assignment: self-assignment (x = x) and adjacent dead reassignment (x = a; x = b; where the second RHS does not reference x). Restricts to plain-identifier LHS only; augmented assignments and member-expression targets are excluded to avoid false positives. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages consulted.

rules.name: no-redundant-boolean
rules.notes: Clean-room port of SonarJS S1125. Reports redundant boolean literals in equality comparisons (==, ===, !=, !==), boolean negation (!true/!false), and ternaries whose both branches are boolean literals. &&/|| with boolean literals are intentionally out of scope. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were copied.

rules.name: no-redundant-jump
rules.notes: Clean-room port of SonarJS S3626. Reports a trailing unlabeled continue; as the last statement of a loop body block (for, while, do-while, for-in, for-of) and a trailing return; (no value) as the last statement of a function body. Labeled continue and valued return are excluded. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-redundant-optional
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S4782). Scope: TSPropertySignature only (interfaces and object type literals). Optional function parameters and optional class fields are out of scope (follow-up). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; no upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-redundant-parentheses
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S1110). Implements only the unambiguous nested double-parenthesis subset: a ParenthesizedExpression whose DIRECT inner child is itself a ParenthesizedExpression ((x)) is flagged, reporting the inner redundant pair. Single-pair precedence-dependent redundancy is intentionally not attempted (zero-FP, under-reports). Relies on the parser's preserve_parens; triple nesting (((x))) yields two reports (one per redundant inner pair). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; no upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-reference-error
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-reference-error (Sonar key S3827). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-referrer-policy
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-referrer-policy (Sonar key S5736). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-regex-spaces
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S6326). Uses the shared regex_ast helper to walk every Alternative's term sequence, detecting maximal runs of two or more consecutive literal space characters (U+0020). Spaces inside character classes are naturally excluded (they live in CharacterClass.body, not Alternative.body). A quantified space ` {n}` is a single Quantifier term, not multiple Character terms, so it is also naturally excluded. Recurses into capturing groups, non-capturing groups, lookaround assertions, and quantifier bodies. No upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-require-or-define
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S3533). Flags bare-identifier CallExpressions whose callee is exactly 'require' or 'define' — the CommonJS loader and AMD module definition patterns. TypeScript import-equals (`import x = require('y')`) is NOT flagged (it parses as TSImportEqualsDeclaration, not a call). Member-expression callees like `foo.require()` are also not flagged. The `module.exports`/`exports.x` CommonJS export assignment forms are deferred as a follow-up (require assignment-target analysis). No upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-return-type-any
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-return-type-any (Sonar key S4324). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-same-argument-assert
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-same-argument-assert (Sonar key S5863). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-same-line-conditional
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S3972). Scans each statement list (program, block, function body, switch case) for an IfStatement whose start line equals the end line of the immediately preceding sibling IfStatement, via check_no_same_line_conditional using LineIndex::loc_for_span. `else if` is part of a single IfStatement (not a sibling) so it is not flagged. No upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-selector-parameter
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-selector-parameter (Sonar key S2301). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-session-cookies-on-static-assets
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-session-cookies-on-static-assets (Sonar key S8441). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-skipped-tests
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S1607). Covers both .skip member form (describe/it/test/context/suite/specify) and x-prefixed Jasmine calls (xit/xdescribe/xtest/xcontext/xspecify). Both forms share the skippedTest messageId. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-small-switch
rules.notes: Clean-room port (S1301). Counts SwitchCase entries with test.is_some() only; default clause (test is None) is excluded from the count. Reports the switch keyword span when real-case count < 2. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages consulted.

rules.name: no-sonar-comments
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S1291). Scans Program.comments for the case-sensitive substring "NOSONAR" and reports one diagnostic per matching comment span. Implemented via check_no_sonar_comments called from visit_program before the AST walk. No upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-tab
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S105). Raw text scan of self.source_text bytes — one diagnostic per source line at the first tab character on that line. Tabs anywhere on a line (including inside strings and comments) are counted. Implemented via check_no_tab called from visit_program before the AST walk. No upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-table-as-layout
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-table-as-layout (Sonar key S5257). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-try-promise
rules.notes: Pending port of upstream rule no-try-promise (Sonar key S4822). SonarJS upstream is LGPL-3.0; this port is clean-room from observed behavior only — never copy upstream source, fixtures, tests, or messages.

rules.name: no-undefined-argument
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S4623). Flags the bare identifier `undefined` when it is the last argument of a CallExpression or NewExpression. Spread arguments and non-last `undefined` are not flagged. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-undefined-assignment
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S2138). Flags AssignmentExpression with operator '=' whose right-hand side is the bare identifier 'undefined'. Variable declarator initializers (let x = undefined) are a documented follow-up. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or message strings were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-unenclosed-multiline-block
rules.notes: Clean-room port (Sonar key S2681). Flags a sibling statement indented as if it belongs to an unbraced control-structure body (if/for/while/else without braces). Column-based indentation check: next sibling column > control keyword column AND >= body statement column, on a later line. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or message strings were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-uniq-key
rules.notes: Clean-room port from public RSPEC S6486 (zero-false-positive subset): flags a JSX key attribute whose value is a Math.random() or Date.now() call, which produces a different key every render and defeats React reconciliation. Index-as-key and cross-render mismatch cases need data-flow analysis and are an intentional under-report. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or message strings were consulted or copied.

rules.name: no-unsafe-unzip
rules.notes: Clean-room port (S5042). Flags a CallExpression whose callee is a static member expression named 'extractAllTo' (adm-zip's whole-archive extraction), which risks a zip-bomb denial of service. Deliberately narrow to the distinctive adm-zip method name to stay zero-false-positive: the secure per-entry 'extractEntryTo' and the generic 'tar.x'/'extract'/'open'/'loadAsync' APIs are intentionally not flagged. No upstream source, tests, fixtures, or messages consulted.

rules.name: blocklist
rules.notes: Rust/Oxc syntax-pattern port of @unocss/eslint-plugin/blocklist for JS/TS/JSX class literals and attributify-style JSX attributes; blocklist entries are passed through rule options or settings.

rules.name: no-unused-imports
rules.notes: Rust/Oxc semantic port of eslint-plugin-unused-imports/no-unused-imports; detects unused ESM import bindings with shadowing-aware references, supports JSDoc tag references, and provides autofixes for import specifiers and whole declarations.

rules.name: no-unused-vars
rules.notes: Rust/Oxc semantic helper rule matching eslint-plugin-unused-imports' split-reporting role by reporting non-import unused bindings separately from no-unused-imports.
